User Job: Find the right batch quickly and understand current stock posture
Capability: Browse, search, sort, and filter inventory batches with live sheet stats
Trigger: Inventory workbook opens or user changes search, sort, or filters
Success Criteria: Batch list, paging, filter chips, and summary stats stay in sync with current inventory state
Current UI Surface: InventoryWorkspacePage -> InventoryWorkSurface table and gallery modes
Validations / Guards: Filter state persists; low stock and age signals stay accurate; refresh path exists
Data Entities: batches; products; suppliers; brands; inventory aggregates
Outputs / Side Effects: Users see current filtered inventory, counts, aging cues, and stock signals
Hidden Dependencies: Saved views; local view state; route telemetry; stock status tokens
Failure Mode if Lost: Users cannot trust what inventory exists or what needs action

Flow Guide Sections: Domain 3.1 Batches; Domain 3.2 Inventory Movements; Domain 3.3 COGS Management
User Job: Open a batch and understand its details before acting

Success Criteria: Inspector shows the correct batch, availability context, and supporting history for the selected record

Validations / Guards: Selection stays synchronized; stale selections clear safely; deep links resolve to the same batch

Evidence Required: Inspector screenshot; route proof; batch history proof

Flow Matrix Rows / Procedures: batches.updateStatus; none found in matrix for inventory.updateStatus current workbook path
User Job: Correct a batch status without breaking auditability
Capability: Update batch status transitions from the inventory sheet
Trigger: User edits status from the inspector, action menu, or edit dialog
Success Criteria: Status changes persist, obey transition rules, surface filter-exit notices, and leave audit evidence with a short undo window
Current UI Surface: InventoryWorkSurface status picker, edit dialog, and sheet actions
Validations / Guards: Valid status transitions; optimistic locking; filter-exit notification; short undo window
Outputs / Side Effects: Batch status changes are saved, audit logged, and can be undone briefly
Hidden Dependencies: Optimistic locking; automatic quarantine synchronization; route filters may hide updated rows; 10-second undo
Failure Mode if Lost: Batch state becomes unsafe or invisible to the wrong users
Sidecar / Exception Need: Inspector for complex status review and reason capture
Parity Test Requirement: State transition E2E plus undo and audit proof
Evidence Required: Mutation proof; audit proof; undo proof; conflict proof

Flow Matrix Rows / Procedures: inventoryMovements.adjust; inventoryMovements.increase; inventoryMovements.decrease; inventoryMovements.validateAvailability; inventoryMovements.reverse; none found in matrix for inventory.adjustQty
User Job: Correct live quantities without breaking audit, stock safety, or status synchronization
Capability: Adjust batch quantity with movement logging, negative-inventory guards, and version checks
Trigger: User opens Adjust Quantity dialog or sheet action
Success Criteria: Quantity changes persist exactly once, movement rows are created, negative inventory is blocked, recent adjustments can be undone, and audit history remains consistent
Validations / Guards: No negative inventory; required reason; optimistic lock; quarantine and on-hand synchronization
Outputs / Side Effects: Batch quantities and movement history stay aligned and are briefly undoable
Hidden Dependencies: Optimistic locking; audit reasons; stock-status recalculation; downstream reservations; 10-second undo
Failure Mode if Lost: Inventory truth diverges from actual stock and audit trail becomes unreliable
Sidecar / Exception Need: Adjustment dialog required for reason capture and conflict handling
Parity Test Requirement: Golden flow inventory adjustment plus race-condition regression
Evidence Required: Dialog proof; audit proof; undo proof; negative guard proof; conflict proof

Flow Matrix Rows / Procedures: batches.updateStatus; batches.delete; none found in matrix for inventory.bulk.updateStatus, inventory.bulk.delete, inventory.bulk.restore
User Job: Update or recover multiple selected batches efficiently
Capability: Bulk update status, soft-delete, and restore selected batches from the sheet
Trigger: User multi-selects rows and triggers a bulk action
Success Criteria: Selected rows update consistently, only zero-on-hand rows can be deleted, partial failures are visible, and recent deletes can be restored
Current UI Surface: InventoryWorkSurface selection model, bulk action bar, undo toast, and confirm dialogs
Validations / Guards: Selection must be explicit; destructive actions require confirmation; delete eligibility depends on zero on-hand quantity; restore must preserve prior status
Outputs / Side Effects: Selected rows update together and recent deletes can be undone or restored when supported
Hidden Dependencies: Selection state; destructive confirmation; prior-status capture for restore; zero-on-hand eligibility; 10-second undo
Failure Mode if Lost: High-volume maintenance becomes slow or unsafe, increasing operator error
Sidecar / Exception Need: Confirm dialogs only for destructive actions
Evidence Required: Bulk update proof; delete eligibility proof; restore proof; partial failure proof

User Job: Trace how stock changed and, when needed, reverse the right movement
Capability: Review movement history, reference traces, and reversal paths for a selected batch
Trigger: User opens history for a selected batch or drills into movement references
Success Criteria: History shows the right records in the right order and reversal paths preserve auditability
Current UI Surface: InventoryWorkSurface inspector/history area or linked review surface
Validations / Guards: History must resolve by batch and by reference; reversals require explicit reason and access
Data Entities: inventory_movements; batches; order and intake references
Outputs / Side Effects: Users can understand and undo the right stock event
Hidden Dependencies: Cross-reference IDs; reversal permissions; movement-type semantics
Failure Mode if Lost: Users lose the ability to explain or correct inventory changes safely
Sidecar / Exception Need: History sidecar or linked review panel required
Evidence Required: History proof; reversal proof; permission proof

User Job: Understand batch value and cost impact from inventory context

Outputs / Side Effects: Users can review cost impact without spreadsheet formulas
Hidden Dependencies: Accounting ownership; reporting surface ownership; downstream margin logic
Failure Mode if Lost: Margin, value, and write-down decisions lose trustworthy context
Sidecar / Exception Need: Likely review/report sidecar or linked accounting surface
Parity Test Requirement: Ownership clarification plus targeted regression once surfaced
Evidence Required: Source reconciliation; eventual UI proof; role proof
